title: ischaemum hispidum
intro: Ischaemum hispidum, also known as the prickly millet grass or wild rice, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Ischaemum hispidum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Ischaemum hispidum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
